data.text: I'll start by exploring the existing MCP tool implementations and HTTP endpoints to understand the patterns.

input.description: Explore MCP tools and HTTP endpoints for docs
input.prompt:
In the repository at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-.execute-bead-wt-ddx-44236615-20260429T185004-ffa9adf1-9d21efb4-20260429T185803-f3a78895, I need to find:

1. Where the existing `ddx_doc_deps` MCP tool is defined/implemented
2. Where the HTTP GET /api/docs/{id}/dependents endpoint is implemented
3. Any other MCP tool implementations that I can use as patterns

Search for "ddx_doc_deps" and "dependents" and look in cli/internal/mcp/ and cli/internal/server/ directories. Show me the file paths and key code sections.

Search breadth: medium

42	// serverPromptCapBytes caps the size of inline prompt bodies accepted on
43	// server-side ingress (POST /api/agent/run). FEAT-022 §9 / Stage D2. It is
44	// a package-level variable rather than a literal so tests can lower the
45	// cap without writing multi-MB request bodies. Production callers must not
46	// mutate it.
47	var serverPromptCapBytes = evidence.DefaultMaxPromptBytes
48	
49	// serverInlineCapBytes caps inline content bodies returned on egress paths
50	// — MCP tool responses and REST JSON responses that carry library-doc,
51	// execution-bundle, diff, session, or persona text. FEAT-022 §10 / §11.
52	// Stage E1. Package-level variable so tests can lower the cap; production
53	// callers must not mutate it.
54	var serverInlineCapBytes = evidence.DefaultMaxInlinedFileBytes
55	
56	// mcpText constructs a text-typed mcpContent whose Text body is bounded by
57	// serverInlineCapBytes via evidence.ClampOutput. The Truncated and
58	// OriginalBytes fields are always populated (false/len(text) when the body
59	// fits within the cap). This is the single mcpContent{Type:"text"} literal
60	// site in this package — every other caller must route through this helper
61	// so static analysis (TestEvidencePrimitiveUsage) can guarantee no
62	// unbounded text egress on the MCP surface. FEAT-022 §10 / Stage E1.
63	func mcpText(text string) mcpContent {
64		clamped, truncated, originalBytes := evidence.ClampOutput(text, serverInlineCapBytes)
65		// evidence:allow-unbounded reason="single mcpContent{Type:\"text\"} literal site for the package; Text is sourced from evidence.ClampOutput above (FEAT-022 §10 Stage E1)"
66		return mcpContent{Type: "text", Text: clamped, Truncated: truncated, OriginalBytes: originalBytes}
67	}
68	
69	// beadHubCloser is the minimal interface the Server requires from its bead
70	// lifecycle hub. *bead.WatcherHub satisfies this interface.
71	type beadHubCloser interface {
72		SubscribeLifecycle(projectID string) (<-chan bead.LifecycleEvent, func())
73		Close()
74	}
75	
76	// Server is the DDx HTTP server exposing REST and MCP endpoints.
77	type Server struct {
78		Addr        string
79		WorkingDir  string
80		TsnetConfig *config.TsnetConfig
81		mux         *http.ServeMux
82		startTime   time.Time
83		workers     *WorkerManager
84		beadHub     beadHubCloser
85		state       *ServerState
86	
87		routePatterns []string // every pattern registered via route(); used by gate tests
88	}
89	
90	// New creates a new DDx server bound to addr, serving data from workingDir.
91	func New(addr, workingDir string) *Server {
92		nodeName := resolveNodeName()
93		stateDir := serverAddrDir() // XDG-standard user-level dir, one per node
94		state := loadServerState(stateDir, nodeName)
95		state.workingDir = workingDir
96	
97		workers := NewWorkerManager(workingDir)
98		workers.ReconcileStaleWorkers()
99		beadHub := bead.NewWatcherHub(250 * time.Millisecond)
100		s := &Server{

21	func (f *CommandFactory) newDocCommand() *cobra.Command {
22		cmd := &cobra.Command{
23			Use:   "doc",
24			Short: "Document dependency graph and staleness tracking",
25			Long: `Manage the document dependency graph.
26	
27	DDx tracks dependencies between documents using YAML frontmatter.
28	When an upstream document changes, DDx detects which downstream
29	documents are stale and need review.
30	
31	Examples:
32	  ddx doc graph              # Show document dependency graph
33	  ddx doc stale              # List stale documents
34	  ddx doc stamp docs/prd.md  # Mark a document as reviewed
35	  ddx doc show helix.prd     # Show document metadata
36	  ddx doc deps helix.arch    # Show what a document depends on
37	  ddx doc dependents helix.prd  # Show what depends on a document`,
38			Aliases: []string{"docs"},
39			PersistentPreRunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
40				serverreg.TryRegisterAsync(f.WorkingDir)
41				return nil
42			},
43			Run: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
44				_ = cmd.Help()
45			},
46		}
47	
48		cmd.AddCommand(f.newDocGraphCommand())
49		cmd.AddCommand(f.newDocStaleCommand())
50		cmd.AddCommand(f.newDocStampCommand())
51		cmd.AddCommand(f.newDocShowCommand())
52		cmd.AddCommand(f.newDocDepsCommand())
53		cmd.AddCommand(f.newDocDependentsCommand())
54		cmd.AddCommand(f.newDocValidateCommand())
55		cmd.AddCommand(f.newDocMigrateCommand())
56		cmd.AddCommand(f.newDocHistoryCommand())
57		cmd.AddCommand(f.newDocDiffCommand())
58		cmd.AddCommand(f.newDocChangedCommand())
59		cmd.AddCommand(f.newDocAuditCommand())
60	
61		return cmd
62	}
63	
64	// newDocAuditCommand surfaces structured integrity issues for CI and
65	// terminal use. Exits 0 when the graph is clean and 1 when any issue is
66	// detected, so CI can gate merges on a healthy graph.
67	func (f *CommandFactory) newDocAuditCommand() *cobra.Command {
68		cmd := &cobra.Command{
69			Use:     "audit",
70			Short:   "Audit document graph integrity (duplicates, missing deps, broken id_to_path)",
71			Aliases: []string{"integrity"},
72			Args:    cobra.NoArgs,
73			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
74				graph, err := f.buildDocGraph()
75				if err != nil {
76					return err
77				}
78				asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
79				exitZero, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("exit-zero")
80				if asJSON {
81					enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())
82					enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
83					if err := enc.Encode(graph.Issues); err != nil {
84						return err
85					}
86					if len(graph.Issues) == 0 || exitZero {
87						return nil
88					}
89					cmd.SilenceUsage = true
90					cmd.SilenceErrors = true
91					return NewExitError(ExitCodeGeneralError, "")
92				}
93				out := cmd.OutOrStdout()
94				if len(graph.Issues) == 0 {
95					fmt.Fprintln(out, "Document graph is clean.")
96					return nil
97				}
98				groups := map[docgraph.IssueKind][]docgraph.GraphIssue{}
99				order := []docgraph.IssueKind{}
100				for _, issue := range graph.Issues {

3034	func (s *Server) mcpCallTool(params json.RawMessage, r *http.Request) mcpToolResult {
3035		var call struct {
3036			Name      string         `json:"name"`
3037			Arguments map[string]any `json:"arguments"`
3038		}
3039		if err := json.Unmarshal(params, &call); err != nil {
3040			return mcpToolResult{
3041				Content: []mcpContent{mcpText("invalid tool call parameters")},
3042				IsError: true,
3043			}
3044		}
3045	
3046		// Tools that operate on project-local data resolve the optional "project"
3047		// argument first; tools listed in the default branch are host/user global
3048		// and ignore project scoping entirely.
3049		project, _ := call.Arguments["project"].(string)
3050	
3051		switch call.Name {
3052		case "ddx_list_projects":
3053			return s.mcpListProjects()
3054		case "ddx_show_project":
3055			id, _ := call.Arguments["id"].(string)
3056			path, _ := call.Arguments["path"].(string)

3058		case "ddx_provider_list":
3059			return s.mcpProviderList()
3060		case "ddx_provider_show":
3061			harness, _ := call.Arguments["harness"].(string)
3062			return s.mcpProviderShow(harness)
3063		}

data.text: All hooks passed and the commit is clean. The `ddx_doc_dependents` MCP tool is now implemented — it mirrors the HTTP `GET /api/docs/{id}/dependents` endpoint by calling `graph.DependentIDs(id)` and returning the reverse dependency list as JSON, enabling agents to do cascade staleness analysis and impact-of-change reasoning.
